[Home Tab \- F3 Nation App](?tab=t.ezcys6v42f0t)  
---

# **📝 Installing the F3 Nation App and Connecting it to your Slack Space**

**Purpose:**  
 This SOP outlines the step-by-step process for installing F3 Nation and connecting to your region’s data

**Platform:**  
Slack (preferably on desktop / web)

---

## **🧭 Step-by-Step Instruction (don’t skip any of these\!)**

## **1\.    Uninstall Slackblast if you have it in your space** ([Remove apps and custom integrations from your workspace | Slack](https://slack.com/help/articles/360003125231-Remove-apps-and-custom-integrations-from-your-workspace)). Between when you install F3 Nation and your migration date, F3 Nation will look and operate exactly like Slackblast did, saving to your paxminer schema, etc. Likely, your guys won’t even notice.  In fact, the Slackblast code exists inside the F3 Nation app and will be running during your transition period.

## **2\.    Paste the [install link](https://slack.com/oauth/v2/authorize?client_id=3242332448050.9191780659751&scope=app_mentions:read,canvases:read,canvases:write,channels:history,channels:join,channels:manage,channels:read,channels:write.invites,channels:write.topic,chat:write,chat:write.customize,chat:write.public,commands,emoji:read,files:read,files:write,groups:history,groups:read,groups:write,groups:write.invites,groups:write.topic,im:history,im:read,im:write,im:write.topic,incoming-webhook,links.embed:write,links:read,links:write,metadata.message:read,mpim:history,mpim:read,mpim:write,mpim:write.topic,pins:read,pins:write,reactions:read,reactions:write,reminders:read,reminders:write,remote_files:read,remote_files:share,remote_files:write,team:read,usergroups:read,usergroups:write,users.profile:read,users:read,users:read.email,users:write&user_scope=) into a browser instance that has a session open to your region’s Slack workspace.**

## **3\.    Make sure your region is selected in the “Workspace” dropdown\!** You can select any channel in the “Channel for webhook” section, it doesn’t matter.

##   ** ![][image1]**

## **4\.    Click “Allow”.**  

### **4.a.    You may get the following message. Click the “here” link.**

###   ** ![A close up of a signAI-generated content may be incorrect.][image2]**

### **4.b.    Click the “Add to Slack” button.**

###    ** ![A screenshot of a applicationAI-generated content may be incorrect.][image3]**

### **4.c.    Again, make sure your region is selected in the “Workspace” dropdown\!**

### **5\.    After clicking “Allow”, it should install in your region’s Slack space.**

### **6\.    From Slack, open settings by typing “/” and then use the “Open F3 Nation Settings” shortcut.** Alternatively you can type the full `/f3-nation-settings` command and hit send.

###    ** ![A screenshot of a computerAI-generated content may be incorrect.][image4]**

### **7\.    If you see a menu option for “Migration Settings”, your slack space is not “connected” to an F3 region in the data system. Open up Migration Settings, and click “connect to an existing region”.**

###    ** ![A screenshot of a computerAI-generated content may be incorrect.][image5]**

### **8\.    Search for your region and select it. Review the following NOTE and select a Migration Date. Hit “Request Connection” once you’ve selected a fitting date.**

**NOTE:** Your chosen migration date is when your data (slackblast/paxminer and qsignups) is migrated to the new data system, where all data will be recorded thereafter. One advantage of choosing a migration date that's a few weeks out: you can continue using the Q signups that Pax have already made in QSignups up until that date, then have them start signing up through F3 Nation after that. Since the QSignups calendar data won't carry over, this gives you a clean cutoff point and avoids having to recreate or manually transfer existing Q commitments.

###    ** ![A screenshot of a computerAI-generated content may be incorrect.][image6]**

### **9\.    You will usually be approved within a day or so, please be patient. Once you get the confirmation message from F3 Nation that your region is connected, you are ready to move on to the next steps.**

### **10\.   \[DO NOT SKIP\!\] Assign AOs to Slack channels or set preblast/backblast channels.**

### If you don’t already, having a Slack channel for each AO has been used successfully by many regions to help build localized community and culture. You can also set preblasts and backblasts to go to single channels, but having one for each AO is strongly encouraged.

### You can do this by going through Settings \-\> Calendar Settings \-\> Manage AOs \-\> Edit or Deactivate AOs, then hit Edit on the dropdowns. Select a Channel to be associated with this AO. You must complete this step before announcing the completion of the migration as the backblast app will error if a user tries to post a backblast for an AO that isn’t associated with a channel.

If you don’t have separate channels for each AO, or you want all of your preblasts and backblasts in single channels, you must set this through Settings \-\> Backblast & Preblast Settings.

### **11\.    Add an “HC” emoji to your space**

F3 Nation relies on having an HC emoji / reaction, which is not a part of the stock Slack emoji pack. Follow [these instructions](https://slack.com/help/articles/206870177-Add-custom-emoji-and-aliases-to-your-workspace) to add custom emoji, and ensure that the HC emoji uses the code `:hc:` . Here is a commonly used image if you need it:  
     ![][image7]

### **\[Optional but recommended\]**

### **12\.    Rename your locations to reflect their “real world” names.**

### In our new data system, we’ve made AOs and Locations independent ideas. Our philosophy:

* An AO is a localized unit of organization and leadership.  
* A Location is… well, a physical location, a set of latitude and longitude.

  ### While many regions define a single AO to be a single Location, some have multiple AOs sharing a Location (ie different days of the week), and some have AOs with multiple Locations (think different parts of a big park).

  Anyway, to help you manage this, we think it will be helpful to rename your Locations to their actual, real world name (like the park name instead of the AO name). This will have secondary benefits to your newer PAX who may not remember where AO names are located.  
    
  You can do this through Settings \-\> Calendar Settings \-\> Manage Locations.

### **What’s Next?**

From here, you can:

1. Uninstall other legacy apps ([Remove apps and custom integrations from your workspace | Slack](https://slack.com/help/articles/360003125231-Remove-apps-and-custom-integrations-from-your-workspace)):  
   * **Paxminer**: your PAX will now be sending preblasts and backblasts through F3 Nation.  
   * **QSignups**: your PAX will now be signing up to Q through F3 Nation / calendar.  
     * **NOTE:** wait until after your chosen migration date to uninstall QSignups \- your PAX will still be able to sign up for Q slots up until then.  
   * **Weaselbot**: though not fully built as of this writing, Weaselbot’s functionality (achievements, Kotter reports, etc.) will be ported to F3 Nation.  
2. Add more region admins through the **\[Region Info\]** submenu\! It will be important to have other users who can help you manage the schedule, edit backblasts, etc.  
3. Set your custom Welcome message in **\[Welcomebot Settings\]** for new PAX to help them navigate your Slack space and learn more about your region.  
4. Browse the other settings to set them to your region’s preferences.  
   * Make sure you go to **\[Reporting Settings\]** and enable monthly reporting if you want. Disclaimer: they are very basic and just point you to PAX Vault.  
5. Request access to our [Google Group](https://groups.google.com/a/f3nation.com/g/f3-data-analytics) to gain access to the BigQuery console, where you can create custom queries and Looker dashboards (guide coming soon\!).  
6. Check out your stats on [https://pax-vault.f3nation.com](https://pax-vault.f3nation.com). You can search for your region and save that URL for later use.
